1→# AgentPress v1 — Build Specification
     2→
     3→**PRD:** `/home/agent/shipyard-ai/prds/prd-agentpress-2026-05-03.md`
     4→**Plan:** `/home/agent/shipyard-ai/.planning/phase-1-plan.md`
     5→**Decisions:** `/home/agent/shipyard-ai/rounds/prd-agentpress-2026-05-03/decisions.md`
     6→**Date:** 2026-05-03
     7→
     8→---
     9→
    10→## 1. Goals (from PRD)
    11→
    12→1. Ship a working WordPress plugin in one session that installs without friction via zip upload or WP CLI.
    13→2. Provide two built-in agents: **ContentWriter**, **ImageGenerator**.
    14→3. Expose an extensible capability registry so third-party developers can add agents (v2, but infrastructure in v1).
    15→4. Route tasks through Claude with a system prompt that matches intent to capability.
    16→5. Demonstrate the full pipeline on our own agency site before public release.
    17→
    18→---
    19→
    20→## 2. Implementation Approach (from Plan)
    21→
    22→### Wave 1 — Foundation (Parallel)
    23→- **Task 1:** Plugin bootstrap with CPT registration (`agentpress_capability`, `agentpress_log`)
    24→- **Task 2:** JSON parser hardening with 10+ edge-case tests
    25→- **Task 3:** Minimal admin CSS (under 100 lines, native WordPress styling)
    26→
    27→### Wave 2 — Core Classes (Parallel)
    28→- **Task 4:** Agents base class + capability registry loader
    29→- **Task 5:** Activity logger with auto-prune (delete oldest 50 when count > 500)
    30→- **Task 6:** ContentWriter agent (Claude API integration)
    31→- **Task 7:** ImageGenerator agent (Cloudflare Workers AI integration)
    32→
    33→### Wave 3 — Orchestration (Sequential)
    34→- **Task 8:** Router with local PHP keyword map + Claude fallback
    35→
    36→### Wave 4 — Integration (Sequential)
    37→- **Task 9:** REST API endpoint `POST /wp-json/agentpress/v1/run`
    38→
    39→### Wave 5 — Admin & Polish (Parallel)
    40→- **Task 10:** Single admin screen (Tools → AgentPress)
    41→- **Task 11:** README + integration smoke test + exclusion audit
    42→
    43→---
    44→
    45→## 3. Verification Criteria
    46→
    47→### 3.1 Plugin Bootstrap (Task 1)
    48→| Criterion | Verification Method |
    49→|-----------|---------------------|
    50→| Plugin activates without fatal errors | Check `wp-content/debug.log` is empty after activation |
    51→| CPT `agentpress_capability` registered | `SELECT post_type FROM wp_posts WHERE post_type = 'agentpress_capability'` returns rows |
    52→| CPT `agentpress_log` registered | `SELECT post_type FROM wp_posts WHERE post_type = 'agentpress_log'` returns rows |
    53→| Default settings option created | `SELECT option_value FROM wp_options WHERE option_name = 'agentpress_settings'` returns serialized array with `default_model = 'claude-3-5-sonnet-20241022'` |
    54→| Built-in capabilities seeded | Query `agentpress_capability` CPT returns `content_writer` and `image_generator` posts |
    55→
    56→### 3.2 JSON Parser (Task 2)
    57→| Criterion | Verification Method |
    58→|-----------|---------------------|
    59→| Parses valid JSON directly | Test case: `{"capability":"content_writer"}` → parsed array |
    60→| Strips markdown fences | Test case: ` ```json {"capability":"x"} ``` ` → parsed array |
    61→| Handles truncated JSON | Test case: `{"capability":"content` → WP_Error with `agentpress_parser_error` |
    62→| Handles hallucinated slugs | Test case: `{"capability":"video_editor"}` → WP_Error (invalid capability) |
    63→| Sanitizes payload strings | Test case: payload with `<script>` tags → `wp_kses_post` strips tags |
    64→
    65→### 3.3 Admin CSS (Task 3)
    66→| Criterion | Verification Method |
    67→|-----------|---------------------|
    68→| File under 100 lines | `wc -l admin/css/agentpress-admin.css` ≤ 100 |
    69→| All selectors prefixed | `grep -v '^\.' admin/css/agentpress-admin.css | grep -v '^\s'` shows only prefixed selectors |
    70→| No framework imports | `grep -i 'tailwind\|bootstrap' admin/css/agentpress-admin.css` returns nothing |
    71→
    72→### 3.4 Agents Registry (Task 4)
    73→| Criterion | Verification Method |
    74→|-----------|---------------------|
    75→| `Agents::get_capabilities()` returns 2 capabilities | Call method, assert count = 2 with slugs `content_writer`, `image_generator` |
    76→| `Agents::build_manifest()` returns serializable array | `json_encode(Agents::build_manifest())` succeeds |
    77→| No public `agentpress_register_capability()` function | `grep -r 'function agentpress_register_capability'` returns nothing |
    78→
    79→### 3.5 Logger (Task 5)
    80→| Criterion | Verification Method |
    81→|-----------|---------------------|
    82→| Log entry created per task | Call `Logger::log()`, verify row in `wp_posts` with post_type `agentpress_log` |
    83→| Auto-prune at 500 entries | Create 505 logs programmatically, verify count ≤ 550 after prune |
    84→| No revisions for log CPT | Query `wp_posts` for `post_type = 'revision'` with parent in `agentpress_log` returns 0 |
    85→
    86→### 3.6 ContentWriter Agent (Task 6)
    87→| Criterion | Verification Method |
    88→|-----------|---------------------|
    89→| Validates required `topic` field | Call with missing `topic` → WP_Error |
    90→| Validates `tone` enum | Call with `tone='casual'` → WP_Error |
    91→| Returns `text` and `tokens_used` | Call with valid payload → array with both keys |
    92→| Truncates to 2048 chars | Mock long response, verify `strlen(text)` ≤ 2048 |
    93→
    94→### 3.7 ImageGenerator Agent (Task 7)
    95→| Criterion | Verification Method |
    96→|-----------|---------------------|
    97→| Validates required `prompt` field | Call with missing `prompt` → WP_Error |
    98→| Validates `size` enum | Call with `size='2048x2048'` → WP_Error |
    99→| Returns HTTPS URL only | Mock `http://` response → WP_Error with HTTPS guardrail message |
   100→| Handles timeout gracefully | Mock unreachable URL → WP_Error with user-friendly timeout message |
   101→
   102→### 3.8 Router (Task 8)
   103→| Criterion | Verification Method |
   104→|-----------|---------------------|
   105→| Local keyword map routes `write` → `content_writer` | Call `Router::route('write a blog post')` → `source='local'`, `capability='content_writer'` |
   106→| Local keyword map routes `image` → `image_generator` | Call `Router::route('generate an image')` → `source='local'`, `capability='image_generator'` |
   107→| Claude fallback for ambiguous tasks | Call `Router::route('something vague')` with valid API key → `source='claude'` |
   108→| Local routing does NOT call Claude | Mock HTTP client, verify no outbound request on local match |
   109→
   110→### 3.9 REST API (Task 9)
   111→| Criterion | Verification Method |
   112→|-----------|---------------------|
   113→| Requires authentication | POST without auth → 401/403 |
   114→| Requires `task` field | POST with empty `task` → 400 `agentpress_missing_task` |
   115→| Returns correct success shape | POST valid task → 200 with `success`, `routing`, `result`, `latency_ms` |
   116→| Logs all executions | After POST, verify `agentpress_log` CPT has new entry |
   117→| Returns structured errors | POST with invalid API key → 500 with `code`, `message`, `data` |
   118→
   119→### 3.10 Admin UI (Task 10)
   120→| Criterion | Verification Method |
   121→|-----------|---------------------|
   122→| Page loads at Tools → AgentPress | Navigate to page, verify no PHP errors |
   123→| No tabs, no manual runner | Visual inspection: no `.nav-tab-wrapper`, no textarea with "Run" button |
   124→| Settings persist | Enter API key, save, reload → value persists |
   125→| Log viewer displays entries | After running tasks, verify table shows Time, Capability, Status, Latency |
   126→| wp-config constants override | Define `AGENTPRESS_CLAUDE_KEY`, verify field disabled with note |
   127→
   128→### 3.11 README + Polish (Task 11)
   129→| Criterion | Verification Method |
   130→|-----------|---------------------|
   131→| readme.txt has valid WP.org header | Header contains `=== AgentPress ===`, `Contributors:`, `Tags:`, `Requires at least:` |
   132→| Three-paragraph description | Count paragraphs in Description section = 3 |
   133→| Exclusion audit passes | `grep -r 'seo_meta\|stream\|async\|queue\|wizard\|billing'` returns no matches in plugin code |
   134→| Smoke test passes | cURL POST to `/wp-json/agentpress/v1/run` → 200, log appears in admin |
   135→
   136→---
   137→
   138→## 4. Files to Create or Modify
   139→
   140→### New Files (11 total)
   141→
   142→| File | Purpose | Wave |
   143→|------|---------|------|
   144→| `agentpress/agentpress.php` | Main plugin file, bootstrap, activation hooks | 1 |
   145→| `agentpress/includes/class-parser.php` | JSON extraction and validation | 1 |
   146→| `agentpress/admin/css/agentpress-admin.css` | Minimal admin styling | 1 |
   147→| `agentpress/includes/class-agents.php` | Capability registry loader | 2 |
   148→| `agentpress/includes/class-logger.php` | CPT activity log with prune | 2 |
   149→| `agentpress/includes/agents/class-content-writer.php` | ContentWriter agent | 2 |
   150→| `agentpress/includes/agents/class-image-generator.php` | ImageGenerator agent | 2 |
   151→| `agentpress/includes/class-router.php` | Keyword map + Claude fallback | 3 |
   152→| `agentpress/includes/class-rest-api.php` | Single REST endpoint | 4 |
   153→| `agentpress/admin/class-admin.php` | Admin screen | 5 |
   154→| `agentpress/readme.txt` | WordPress.org readme | 5 |
   155→
   156→### Third Agent (1 file)
   157→
   158→| File | Purpose |
   159→|------|---------|
   160→| `agentpress/includes/agents/class-seo-meta.php` | SEO Meta agent — generates title tags and meta descriptions via Claude API |
   161→
   162→### Modified Files
   163→
   164→None — this is a net-new plugin with no existing file modifications.
   165→
   166→---
   167→
   168→## 5. File Structure
   169→
   170→```
   171→agentpress/
   172→├── agentpress.php
   173→├── includes/
   174→│   ├── class-parser.php
   175→│   ├── class-agents.php
   176→│   ├── class-logger.php
   177→│   ├── class-router.php
   178→│   ├── class-rest-api.php
   179→│   └── agents/
   180→│       ├── class-content-writer.php
   181→│       ├── class-image-generator.php
   182→│       └── class-agent-third.php
   183→├── admin/
   184→│   ├── class-admin.php
   185→│   └── css/
   186→│       └── agentpress-admin.css
   187→└── readme.txt
   188→```
   189→
   190→---
   191→
   192→## 6. Key Constraints
   193→
   194→1. **Two agents only:** ContentWriter and ImageGenerator. SEOMeta is explicitly excluded.
   195→2. **No manual task runner UI:** Test via cURL/Postman only.
   196→3. **No tabs in admin:** Single screen under Tools → AgentPress.
   197→4. **No encrypted storage theater:** Standard options or wp-config constants only.
   198→5. **CPT-based logging with auto-prune:** Delete oldest 50 when count > 500.
   199→6. **Local keyword map first:** Claude fallback only for ambiguous intent.
   200→7. **Synchronous REST only:** No streaming, no async queues.
   201→8. **Native WordPress styling:** No Tailwind, no Bootstrap, under 100 lines CSS.
   202→

file.content:
# AgentPress v1 — Build Specification

**PRD:** `/home/agent/shipyard-ai/prds/prd-agentpress-2026-05-03.md`
**Plan:** `/home/agent/shipyard-ai/.planning/phase-1-plan.md`
**Decisions:** `/home/agent/shipyard-ai/rounds/prd-agentpress-2026-05-03/decisions.md`
**Date:** 2026-05-03

---

## 1. Goals (from PRD)

1. Ship a working WordPress plugin in one session that installs without friction via zip upload or WP CLI.
2. Provide two built-in agents: **ContentWriter**, **ImageGenerator**.
3. Expose an extensible capability registry so third-party developers can add agents (v2, but infrastructure in v1).
4. Route tasks through Claude with a system prompt that matches intent to capability.
5. Demonstrate the full pipeline on our own agency site before public release.

---

## 2. Implementation Approach (from Plan)

### Wave 1 — Foundation (Parallel)
- **Task 1:** Plugin bootstrap with CPT registration (`agentpress_capability`, `agentpress_log`)
- **Task 2:** JSON parser hardening with 10+ edge-case tests
- **Task 3:** Minimal admin CSS (under 100 lines, native WordPress styling)

### Wave 2 — Core Classes (Parallel)
- **Task 4:** Agents base class + capability registry loader
- **Task 5:** Activity logger with auto-prune (delete oldest 50 when count > 500)
- **Task 6:** ContentWriter agent (Claude API integration)
- **Task 7:** ImageGenerator agent (Cloudflare Workers AI integration)

### Wave 3 — Orchestration (Sequential)
- **Task 8:** Router with local PHP keyword map + Claude fallback

### Wave 4 — Integration (Sequential)
- **Task 9:** REST API endpoint `POST /wp-json/agentpress/v1/run`

### Wave 5 — Admin & Polish (Parallel)
- **Task 10:** Single admin screen (Tools → AgentPress)
- **Task 11:** README + integration smoke test + exclusion audit

---

## 3. Verification Criteria

### 3.1 Plugin Bootstrap (Task 1)
| Criterion | Verification Method |
|-----------|---------------------|
| Plugin activates without fatal errors | Check `wp-content/debug.log` is empty after activation |
| CPT `agentpress_capability` registered | `SELECT post_type FROM wp_posts WHERE post_type = 'agentpress_capability'` returns rows |
| CPT `agentpress_log` registered | `SELECT post_type FROM wp_posts WHERE post_type = 'agentpress_log'` returns rows |
| Default settings option created | `SELECT option_value FROM wp_options WHERE option_name = 'agentpress_settings'` returns serialized array with `default_model = 'claude-3-5-sonnet-20241022'` |
| Built-in capabilities seeded | Query `agentpress_capability` CPT returns `content_writer` and `image_generator` posts |

### 3.2 JSON Parser (Task 2)
| Criterion | Verification Method |
|-----------|---------------------|
| Parses valid JSON directly | Test case: `{"capability":"content_writer"}` → parsed array |
| Strips markdown fences | Test case: ` ```json {"capability":"x"} ``` ` → parsed array |
| Handles truncated JSON | Test case: `{"capability":"content` → WP_Error with `agentpress_parser_error` |
| Handles hallucinated slugs | Test case: `{"capability":"video_editor"}` → WP_Error (invalid capability) |
| Sanitizes payload strings | Test case: payload with `<script>` tags → `wp_kses_post` strips tags |

### 3.3 Admin CSS (Task 3)
| Criterion | Verification Method |
|-----------|---------------------|
| File under 100 lines | `wc -l admin/css/agentpress-admin.css` ≤ 100 |
| All selectors prefixed | `grep -v '^\.' admin/css/agentpress-admin.css | grep -v '^\s'` shows only prefixed selectors |
| No framework imports | `grep -i 'tailwind\|bootstrap' admin/css/agentpress-admin.css` returns nothing |

### 3.4 Agents Registry (Task 4)
| Criterion | Verification Method |
|-----------|---------------------|
| `Agents::get_capabilities()` returns 2 capabilities | Call method, assert count = 2 with slugs `content_writer`, `image_generator` |
| `Agents::build_manifest()` returns serializable array | `json_encode(Agents::build_manifest())` succeeds |
| No public `agentpress_register_capability()` function | `grep -r 'function agentpress_register_capability'` returns nothing |

### 3.5 Logger (Task 5)
| Criterion | Verification Method |
|-----------|---------------------|
| Log entry created per task | Call `Logger::log()`, verify row in `wp_posts` with post_type `agentpress_log` |
| Auto-prune at 500 entries | Create 505 logs programmatically, verify count ≤ 550 after prune |
| No revisions for log CPT | Query `wp_posts` for `post_type = 'revision'` with parent in `agentpress_log` returns 0 |

### 3.6 ContentWriter Agent (Task 6)
| Criterion | Verification Method |
|-----------|---------------------|
| Validates required `topic` field | Call with missing `topic` → WP_Error |
| Validates `tone` enum | Call with `tone='casual'` → WP_Error |
| Returns `text` and `tokens_used` | Call with valid payload → array with both keys |
| Truncates to 2048 chars | Mock long response, verify `strlen(text)` ≤ 2048 |

### 3.7 ImageGenerator Agent (Task 7)
| Criterion | Verification Method |
|-----------|---------------------|
| Validates required `prompt` field | Call with missing `prompt` → WP_Error |
| Validates `size` enum | Call with `size='2048x2048'` → WP_Error |
| Returns HTTPS URL only | Mock `http://` response → WP_Error with HTTPS guardrail message |
| Handles timeout gracefully | Mock unreachable URL → WP_Error with user-friendly timeout message |

### 3.8 Router (Task 8)
| Criterion | Verification Method |
|-----------|---------------------|
| Local keyword map routes `write` → `content_writer` | Call `Router::route('write a blog post')` → `source='local'`, `capability='content_writer'` |
| Local keyword map routes `image` → `image_generator` | Call `Router::route('generate an image')` → `source='local'`, `capability='image_generator'` |
| Claude fallback for ambiguous tasks | Call `Router::route('something vague')` with valid API key → `source='claude'` |
| Local routing does NOT call Claude | Mock HTTP client, verify no outbound request on local match |

### 3.9 REST API (Task 9)
| Criterion | Verification Method |
|-----------|---------------------|
| Requires authentication | POST without auth → 401/403 |
| Requires `task` field | POST with empty `task` → 400 `agentpress_missing_task` |
| Returns correct success shape | POST valid task → 200 with `success`, `routing`, `result`, `latency_ms` |
| Logs all executions | After POST, verify `agentpress_log` CPT has new entry |
| Returns structured errors | POST with invalid API key → 500 with `code`, `message`, `data` |

### 3.10 Admin UI (Task 10)
| Criterion | Verification Method |
|-----------|---------------------|
| Page loads at Tools → AgentPress | Navigate to page, verify no PHP errors |
| No tabs, no manual runner | Visual inspection: no `.nav-tab-wrapper`, no textarea with "Run" button |
| Settings persist | Enter API key, save, reload → value persists |
| Log viewer displays entries | After running tasks, verify table shows Time, Capability, Status, Latency |
| wp-config constants override | Define `AGENTPRESS_CLAUDE_KEY`, verify field disabled with note |

### 3.11 README + Polish (Task 11)
| Criterion | Verification Method |
|-----------|---------------------|
| readme.txt has valid WP.org header | Header contains `=== AgentPress ===`, `Contributors:`, `Tags:`, `Requires at least:` |
| Three-paragraph description | Count paragraphs in Description section = 3 |
| Exclusion audit passes | `grep -r 'seo_meta\|stream\|async\|queue\|wizard\|billing'` returns no matches in plugin code |
| Smoke test passes | cURL POST to `/wp-json/agentpress/v1/run` → 200, log appears in admin |

---

## 4. Files to Create or Modify

### New Files (11 total)

| File | Purpose | Wave |
|------|---------|------|
| `agentpress/agentpress.php` | Main plugin file, bootstrap, activation hooks | 1 |
| `agentpress/includes/class-parser.php` | JSON extraction and validation | 1 |
| `agentpress/admin/css/agentpress-admin.css` | Minimal admin styling | 1 |
| `agentpress/includes/class-agents.php` | Capability registry loader | 2 |
| `agentpress/includes/class-logger.php` | CPT activity log with prune | 2 |
| `agentpress/includes/agents/class-content-writer.php` | ContentWriter agent | 2 |
| `agentpress/includes/agents/class-image-generator.php` | ImageGenerator agent | 2 |
| `agentpress/includes/class-router.php` | Keyword map + Claude fallback | 3 |
| `agentpress/includes/class-rest-api.php` | Single REST endpoint | 4 |
| `agentpress/admin/class-admin.php` | Admin screen | 5 |
| `agentpress/readme.txt` | WordPress.org readme | 5 |

### Third Agent (1 file)

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `agentpress/includes/agents/class-seo-meta.php` | SEO Meta agent — generates title tags and meta descriptions via Claude API |

### Modified Files

None — this is a net-new plugin with no existing file modifications.

---

## 5. File Structure

```
agentpress/
├── agentpress.php
├── includes/
│   ├── class-parser.php
│   ├── class-agents.php
│   ├── class-logger.php
│   ├── class-router.php
│   ├── class-rest-api.php
│   └── agents/
│       ├── class-content-writer.php
│       ├── class-image-generator.php
│       └── class-agent-third.php
├── admin/
│   ├── class-admin.php
│   └── css/
│       └── agentpress-admin.css
└── readme.txt
```

---

## 6. Key Constraints

1. **Two agents only:** ContentWriter and ImageGenerator. SEOMeta is explicitly excluded.
2. **No manual task runner UI:** Test via cURL/Postman only.
3. **No tabs in admin:** Single screen under Tools → AgentPress.
4. **No encrypted storage theater:** Standard options or wp-config constants only.
5. **CPT-based logging with auto-prune:** Delete oldest 50 when count > 500.
6. **Local keyword map first:** Claude fallback only for ambiguous intent.
7. **Synchronous REST only:** No streaming, no async queues.
8. **Native WordPress styling:** No Tailwind, no Bootstrap, under 100 lines CSS.

content.thinking:
Now I understand the product: AgentPress is a WordPress plugin that adds AI agents (ContentWriter and ImageGenerator) to a WordPress site via a REST API. It routes tasks through Claude with a local keyword map fallback.

Now I need to write a 2-minute demo script in Aaron Sorkin style. Let me think about what makes Sorkin dialogue distinctive:
- Rapid-fire, witty, intelligent dialogue
- Walking and talking
- Personal stakes, urgency
- Characters who are very competent and under pressure
- "You had me at hello" type moments but with more words
- Often opens with someone already in motion dealing with a crisis

The product is a WordPress plugin that lets you give AI tasks via API. The "personal problem" could be: a content manager, a developer, or agency owner drowning in content requests at 11 PM before a launch.

For a 2-minute demo script with NARRATOR + [SCREEN:] directions, I need to make it feel like a scene. But wait — the user asked for a "human narrator voice" and "NARRATOR lines + [SCREEN: description] stage directions." So it's a voiceover demo script, not character dialogue. But I need to infuse it with Sorkin energy — rhythm, urgency, intelligence, momentum.

Let me craft this:

OPEN WITH THE PROBLEM:
- It's late. Launch tomorrow. Client needs 12 blog posts and hero images. Writer is sick. You're alone.

SHOW THE PRODUCT SOLVING IT:
- Install AgentPress (zip upload)
- Set API key
- Send one REST request: "Write a blog post about sustainable packaging"
- Watch it appear as a draft
- Send another: "Generate a hero image, minimalist, green"
- Image appears in media library

END WITH THE WOW MOMENT:
- Not that it did one task. That it understood what you meant without you being a developer. That you just... asked. And WordPress listened.

Wait, actually looking at the spec, there is no "media library upload" mentioned for images — it returns an HTTPS URL. And ContentWriter returns text. So the wow moment is: one endpoint, plain English, routed to the right agent automatically. The router is the magic.

Let me write this with Sorkin cadence — staccato sentences, building momentum, a turn in the middle where things start working, ending on a powerful final line.

Actually, let me re-read the user's format request carefully:
"Format: NARRATOR lines + [SCREEN: description] stage directions."

So:
NARRATOR
Line of narration.

[SCREEN: Description of what's on screen]

I need to make it ~2 minutes. At a brisk narration pace, maybe 140-160 words per minute, so roughly 280-320 words total. With Sorkin style, the narrator talks fast.

Let me draft:

---

NARRATOR
It's eleven fourteen. Your writer has the flu. Launch is in nine hours and the client just added—don't they always—a dozen blog posts and hero images to the scope. You're staring at WordPress like it owes you money.

[SCREEN: Dark bedroom. Laptop glow on tired face. WordPress dashboard open, cursor blinking on empty "Add New Post" screen.]

NARRATOR
You don't need more coffee. You need an agent.

[SCREEN: Quick zip upload of AgentPress plugin. Activate. Tools → AgentPress. API key field.]

NARRATOR
Upload. Activate. One API key. Done.

[SCREEN: Terminal window. Hands type: curl -X POST /wp-json/agentpress/v1/run -d '{"task":"Write a 500-word post on sustainable packaging, professional tone"}']

NARRATOR
Now watch. You don't write a spec. You don't file a ticket. You type one sentence in plain English and hit return.

[SCREEN: Response JSON appears. Cut to WordPress Posts list — new draft appears: "Sustainable Packaging: A Professional Overview". Word count: 487.]

NARRATOR
Router sees "write," sends it to ContentWriter. Article's in your drafts before you exhale.

[SCREEN: Another curl. Task: "Hero image, minimalist, green leaves, 1024x1024."]

NARRATOR
Now you need an image. Same endpoint. Same sentence.

[SCREEN: Response JSON with HTTPS image URL. Cut to Media Library — new image thumbnail loads. Clean, minimalist, green.]

NARRATOR
ImageGenerator picks it up. No routing. No dropdown menus. The plugin understood what you meant.

[SCREEN: Admin log viewer. Table fills: Time, Capability, Status, Latency. 1.2 seconds. 1.4 seconds. Green checks.]

NARRATOR
Every request, logged. Every route, visible. Under two seconds.

[SCREEN: Quick montage: three more posts publish. Final image uploads. Clock on screen: 11:47.]

NARRATOR
Eight posts and four images in thirty-three minutes. And here's the thing—

[SCREEN: Final shot. Morning. Sunlight. Same laptop. Client email: "Looks amazing. Ship it." User smiles, closes lid.]

NARRATOR

For a demo script, the "wow" is the speed and the routing. But to make it visual and personal, I can show the user taking the result and using it. That's actually more honest and still powerful. The Sorkin narrator can frame it as "you asked, you got it, you publish."
